burden.source: IHME/WHO sleep and neurological disorder context

vulnerablePopulations: patients with delayed access to sleep medicine diagnostics
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Excessive daytime sleepiness with cataplexy (type 1) and confirmation by PSG + MSLT (mean sleep latency ≤8 min with ≥2 SOREMPs) or CSF hypocretin when available

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; medication and specialist access determine symptom control

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: PSG/MSLT availability limited and concentrated in larger centers; diagnosis delays are common
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Sleep hygiene and scheduled naps; wake-promoting agents/stimulants and anticataplectic therapy where available; comorbidity management
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access to sleep labs and specialist care; medication coverage variability
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Tertiary centers can provide diagnostic testing and follow-up in major cities
